top_claims.claim: Deutsche Börse is a Stahl croupier at about €246 per share: we expect about 14.48% per year over 7 years on mid-cycle free cash flow per share (€12.52), below our about-fifteen-percent-per-year target (bear about 8%, bull about 18%). FY2025 filings confirm €5,189 million net revenue excluding treasury (+9%), €2,675 mil
We expect **14.48%** per year over 7 years at **~$246.4** on **$12.52/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Treasury result compression as ECB and global rates fall, masking operating momentum and preventing multiple expansion.
Trading volume mean-reversion after Q1 2026 spike in rates and commodities derivatives.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Treasury result compression as ECB and global rates fall, masking operating momentum and preventing multiple expansion.
Trading volume mean-reversion after Q1 2026 spike in rates and commodities derivatives.
Allfunds integration (regulatory approvals, synergy timing, financing) and €1.1 billion ISS STOXX buyout cash drain in Q1 2026.
